title: Drone captures 'mind boggling' shots of lava in volcano crater - ABC News
creator: ABC News
notes: CANDIDATE-SOURCED, INDEPENDENTLY RE-VERIFIED THIS SESSION -- first exemplar added to the volcanic-landscapes subgenre bucket (lava fields, craters, volcanic peaks), a subject not previously represented in this pack. A prior agent's candidate JSON proposed this URL (https://www.youtube.com/shorts/0JhuyXMMNig); per this task's step 1 requirement, it was NOT trusted verbatim -- it was re-verified live in this session via YouTube's oEmbed endpoint (curl 'https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/shorts/0JhuyXMMNig&format=json'), which returned title "Drone captures 'mind boggling' shots of lava in volcano crater - ABC News", author_name 'ABC News', author_url 'https://www.youtube.com/@ABCNews', and embed dimensions height=200/width=113 -- an exact field-for-field match to the candidate's claimed title, creator, and vertical-Shorts framing, so this session's own primary-source check corroborates rather than merely repeats the prior agent's claim. No Creative Commons marking of any kind was present in the oEmbed response (no licensedForReuse/cc-by field), so per this project's stated default (assume all-rights-reserved unless the uploader's license setting is directly confirmed otherwise), license_category is set to 'all-rights-reserved' -- consistent with ABC News being a conventional broadcaster channel with standard copyright, not a CC-licensed uploader. Consequently local_media_path stays null and no video file was downloaded or persisted anywhere in this repo. duration is left null: YouTube's oEmbed response does not carry a duration field, and no further verification of runtime (YouTube Data API call or manual playback) was attempted in this session -- rather than fabricate a plausible Shorts-length number, this field stays unset. award_or_showcase_provenance is left null: this is a news-broadcaster segment/repost of dramatic footage, not a formally juried contest or festival selection, and no award claim was found or surfaced anywhere in the oEmbed metadata or the prior agent's research -- inventing one would violate the no-fabrication constraint. Subject-distinctiveness note (carried forward from the prior agent's verification_notes, and independently plausible given the ABC News branding versus the other two raw candidates it names): this URL was selected over two Iceland-specific lava-eruption candidates (AccuWeather, IFLScience) to avoid a near-duplicate 'Iceland eruption lava flow' subject within the volcanic subgenre slot and to maximize footage/location distinctiveness across this pack -- this session did not re-verify the two rejected candidates itself (out of scope for a single-record task), so that comparative claim is attributed to the prior agent's research rather than asserted as independently confirmed here. scores (sharpness/exposure/motion_smoothness) are scores_provenance: manually_estimated placeholder judgments, NOT measurements from Capability 1's actual scoring functions (scoring_sharpness.py/scoring_exposure.py/scoring_motion_smoothness.py) -- no source file was ever downloaded to run those functions against, per the spec's no-bulk-download constraint. Calibration reasoning, consistent with this pack's existing ~0.70-0.85 range: sharpness=0.77 (a professional news-broadcaster repost implies a competent camera/drone platform and standard broadcast-quality re-encode, comparable to this pack's other broadcaster-sourced exemplars such as bbc-earth-aerial.json's 0.78 and skypixel-landscape.json's 0.78, but nudged slightly down from that tier because volcanic-crater environments are a documented source of atmospheric heat-haze/shimmer distortion that measurably softens apparent per-frame detail regardless of sensor quality -- a genre-specific optical challenge, not a guess); exposure=0.72 (an active lava crater is a genuinely harder dynamic-range case than this pack's daylight-landscape exemplars: incandescent molten lava (extremely bright, localized highlights) against dark volcanic rock and typically dim ambient/night conditions is a classic high-contrast scene prone to either clipped lava highlights or crushed shadow detail on the surrounding crater walls, and no HDR/log-profile handling claim was found to offset that -- scored near this pack's low-to-mid tier, comparable to lido-wave-nazare-coastal.json's 0.70 sunset-backlit case); motion_smoothness=0.78 (the clip is described, and the vertical-Shorts format is consistent with, a single continuous FPV descent shot rather than a multi-angle edit, and a deliberate, camera-forward descent into a hazardous crater plausibly reflects a piloted, considered flight line rather than erratic handheld-feeling motion -- scored at this pack's upper-middle tier, similar to drone-racing-league-waterfall-fpv-dive.json's 0.80 single-dive FPV shot, but not at that record's exact level since no explicit gyro-stabilization-software claim, e.g. ReelSteady/Gyroflow, was found for this specific clip, and thermal updrafts around active volcanic vents are a plausible, if undocumented, source of additional flight turbulence). composite_score IS a real computation: it was produced this session by calling this repo's own drone_video_ai.highlight_extraction.composite.compute_composite_score with these three manually-estimated inputs (sharpness=0.77, exposure=0.72, motion_smoothness=0.78, composition=None) and drone_video_ai.highlight_extraction.weights.default_weights() via '.venv/bin/python3' in this session. default_weights() with no explicit version argument returned weights_version 'default-v2' (sharpness=exposure=motion_smoothness=composition=0.25 each); because composition's score is None (this pack's Milestone-1 Capability-1 convention), compute_composite_score's defensive None-skipping behavior excludes composition from both the weighted sum and the renormalization denominator, so the result reduces to the equal-weighted average of the three present signals: (0.77+0.72+0.78)/3 = 0.7566666666666667 (rounded to 0.7567 here), confirmed by actually running the function this session rather than hand-computed. editorial_style: format='short-form-reel' is grounded in structural facts obtained via this session's own oEmbed fetch, not a guess from the title alone -- (1) the source URL itself is a /shorts/ path, YouTube's own platform-level designation for short-form vertical content; (2) the oEmbed response's embed dimensions (height=200, width=113) describe a portrait/vertical aspect ratio, inconsistent with the 16:9 landscape framing of this pack's long-form cinematic exemplars -- the same structural-evidence basis this pack already uses in drone-racing-league-waterfall-fpv-dive.json. No live playback or scrubbing of the video was attempted in this session (no browser-automation tool was invoked), so review_method is set to 'text_provenance_only' rather than 'live_playback_review', and estimated_cut_count/avg_shot_length_seconds/transition_styles_observed stay null/empty rather than guessed, consistent with the prior agent's editorial-review pass finding no cut-count or shot-length detail in any source consulted.

Given a punchy single-shot news-style FPV descent is the described content, it plausibly has few or no internal cuts, but that is an inference from genre/format convention, not an observation, so it is not asserted as a specific cut count.
